metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the AC activation terminology with the actual codebase mechanism: replace 'gerrit_voter.enabled=true, project allow-listed' with the concrete env-var steps (e.g. `GERRIT_PROJECT=rebar` already set in `config.py:113`) or confirm whether a new `[gerrit_voter]` TOML section is intentionally being introduced by this ticket.
findings.finding: The plan's AC coins a config-surface key ('gerrit_voter.enabled=true', '[gerrit_voter] staged opt-in') that does not exist anywhere in the codebase. The actual activation mechanism is the GERRIT_PROJECT env var (config.py:113) plus the Docker Compose deployment — no TOML section or boolean toggle named 'gerrit_voter' exists in rebar.toml, config.py, or any infra file. Referencing a nonexistent config key risks confusion about what 'activation' means in the live deployment.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'gerrit_voter.enabled=true, project allow-listed'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC line with: 'The WS6-a voter is deployed and the `GERRIT_PROJECT=rebar` env var (plus any compose `.env` entries from `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh`) is confirmed set on the live receiver — no new config key needed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the fabricated `gerrit_voter.enabled=true` / `[gerrit_voter]` activation language in AC1 and the 'What' section with the real mechanism: confirm `GERRIT_PROJECT=rebar` (or verify the default in `ReceiverConfig.project`) is set in the deployed container environment, and that the receiver is reachable at `/review/webhook`.
findings.finding: The plan's AC1 references a non-existent activation mechanism: '`gerrit_voter.enabled=true`, project allow-listed'. No config key `gerrit_voter.enabled`, no `[gerrit_voter]` section, and no 'staged opt-in' concept exist anywhere in the codebase. The real activation knob is the `GERRIT_PROJECT` env var in `ReceiverConfig` (config.py:79,113) which filters by exact project name match (voter.py:151). The plan's stated activation path cannot be followed as written.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'The WS6-a voter is deployed + activated for the rebar Gerrit project (`gerrit_voter.enabled=true`, project allow-listed)'
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC1 as: 'The WS6-a voter is deployed with `GERRIT_PROJECT=rebar` set in the container environment (the default in `ReceiverConfig`); the receiver is running and reachable at `https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/review/health`; and Gerrit's webhooks.config routes `patchset-created` events to `/review/webhook`.' Remove all references to `gerrit_voter.enabled` and `[gerrit_voter]` — these config keys do not exist.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the hallucinated `gerrit_voter.enabled=true` / `[gerrit_voter]` activation mechanism in AC1 with the actual knob: confirm `GERRIT_PROJECT=rebar` is set in the `.env` (or SSM-sourced) for the `review-bot` container, matching the project filter in `ReceiverConfig.project` (`config.py:113`).
findings.finding: The plan's AC1 references `gerrit_voter.enabled=true` and a `[gerrit_voter]` staged opt-in as the voter activation mechanism, but neither symbol nor config section exists anywhere in the codebase (src/ or infra/). The actual activation mechanism is the `GERRIT_PROJECT` environment variable (mapped to `ReceiverConfig.project` in `config.py:113`); the voter silently skips events whose `change.project` doesn't match this value (`voter.py:151`). There is no `enabled` toggle.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1: 'gerrit_voter.enabled=true, project allow-listed'
findings.suggested_fix: AC1 should read: 'The WS6-a voter is deployed (review-bot container running) and the `GERRIT_PROJECT` env var is set to `rebar` so the voter processes rebar changes (voter.py:151).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct 'rebar-MCP receiver' to 'review-bot webhook receiver' (the `review-bot` docker-compose service running `rebar.review_bot.app`) to match the actual deployment target named in `docker-compose.yml` and `app.py`.
findings.finding: The plan describes deploying the voter 'into the rebar-MCP receiver', but no component called 'rebar-MCP receiver' exists. The actual deployment target is the `review-bot` FastAPI ASGI service (`rebar.review_bot.app`, running via uvicorn in the `review-bot` docker-compose service). The MCP server (`rebar.mcp_server`) is a completely separate entrypoint; `docker-compose.yml` line 79–80 explicitly documents that `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM` is deliberately NOT set because 'the receiver calls the rebar.llm library directly'.
findings.location: What section: 'Deploy the WS6-a voter into the rebar-MCP receiver on the provisioned AWS/Gerrit environment'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'rebar-MCP receiver' with 'review-bot webhook receiver (the `review-bot` docker-compose service, `rebar.review_bot.app`)' throughout the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the AC and 'What' section: replace the non-existent `gerrit_voter.enabled=true` / `[gerrit_voter]` staged opt-in language with the actual activation mechanism — deploying the receiver container with `GERRIT_PROJECT=rebar` (and the bot credentials) in the live environment, which is already the `ReceiverConfig` default.
findings.finding: The AC says `gerrit_voter.enabled=true` (project allow-listed) is the activation toggle, but this config key does not exist anywhere in the codebase. The real activation mechanism is env-var–driven: the receiver is activated for a project by deploying the container with `GERRIT_PROJECT=rebar` (the `ReceiverConfig.project` field, default `'rebar'`). There is no `[gerrit_voter]` section in `rebar.toml` or any config file, and no `gerrit_voter.enabled` flag. An implementer following the AC literally would look for (or introduce) a non-existent configuration knob, diverging from the actual deployment mechanism.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — first bullet: `gerrit_voter.enabled=true`, project allow-listed
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'activate it for the rebar project via the `[gerrit_voter]` staged opt-in' with 'confirm the receiver is deployed with `GERRIT_PROJECT=rebar` (the default) and the bot credentials provisioned in SSM' — matching the actual env-var–driven ReceiverConfig activation model.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the non-existent `gerrit_voter.enabled=true` / `[gerrit_voter]` terminology in AC1 and the What section with the actual activation mechanism (e.g. the receiver is deployed and running, `adapter.py` passes `enabled=True` to `produce_code_review_verdict`, and the Gerrit project's `project.config` allows the bot account to vote `LLM-Review` — as configured in d251/S3's `infra/gerrit/project.config`) so the AC is verifiable against real artifacts.
findings.finding: The plan's AC1 and What section refer to a '[gerrit_voter] staged opt-in' with 'gerrit_voter.enabled=true' and 'project allow-listed' as concrete, verifiable config keys, but no such config section or key exists anywhere in the codebase — a repo-wide search for 'gerrit_voter' in src/ returns zero results. The actual activation mechanism (per ADR-0015) is the adapter passing enabled=True to produce_code_review_verdict plus the receiver being deployed and configured. The mismatch between the plan's named config key and the real activation surface is an unverified capability assertion: if an implementer follows the AC literally (setting 'gerrit_voter.enabled=true'), they will find no such knob.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 ('gerrit_voter.enabled=true, project allow-listed'); What section ('[gerrit_voter] staged opt-in')
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC1 as: 'The WS6-a voter is deployed (review-bot container running on the AWS instance) and wired to the rebar Gerrit project: the adapter passes `enabled=True` to `produce_code_review_verdict` (per ADR-0015 §2), the bot account is in the `Service Users` group with `label-LLM-Review = -1..+1` permission (per `infra/gerrit/project.config`), and the receiver is reachable at `/review/` on `rebar.solutions.navateam.com`.' Drop the `[gerrit_voter]` staged opt-in framing, which refers to no real config surface.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the non-existent `gerrit_voter.enabled=true` / `[gerrit_voter]` reference in AC1 with the actual activation mechanism: confirm `GERRIT_BOT_TOKEN` and `GERRIT_PROJECT=rebar` are set via SSM + `fetch-secrets.sh`, and that the webhooks.config `patchset-created` event is wired to the receiver.
findings.finding: AC1 names a non-existent activation mechanism ('gerrit_voter.enabled=true', '[gerrit_voter] staged opt-in') that does not exist anywhere in the codebase. The real activation surface is environment variables (GERRIT_PROJECT, GERRIT_BOT_TOKEN, etc.) sourced from SSM via infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh, not a config-file toggle. The AC as written cannot be verified against the actual system.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'The WS6-a voter is deployed + activated for the rebar Gerrit project (`gerrit_voter.enabled=true`, project allow-listed).'
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC1 to: 'The WS6-a voter is deployed on the live AWS host, `GERRIT_PROJECT=rebar` and `GERRIT_BOT_TOKEN` are populated from SSM (`/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token`), and the Gerrit webhooks plugin is delivering `patchset-created` events to the receiver (confirmed via `reviewbot-e2e.sh` or equivalent).' This matches the actual activation surface in `src/rebar/review_bot/config.py` and `infra/gerrit/webhooks.config`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the fictional `gerrit_voter.enabled=true` / `[gerrit_voter] staged opt-in` language in AC1 with the actual activation surface: the receiver process is running on AWS with `GERRIT_PROJECT=rebar` and valid `GERRIT_BOT_TOKEN` in the compose `.env`, and the `project.config` ACL grants `label-LLM-Review` to `Service Users` (verifiable via `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` + `infra/gerrit/project.config`).
findings.finding: AC1 references 'gerrit_voter.enabled=true' and the '[gerrit_voter] staged opt-in' as a named, verifiable activation mechanism, but no such config key, section, or opt-in surface exists anywhere in the codebase. The real activation mechanism is entirely environment-variable-driven (GERRIT_PROJECT, GERRIT_BOT_TOKEN, GERRIT_BASE_URL, etc. via ReceiverConfig.from_env() in src/rebar/review_bot/config.py). An executor trying to verify AC1 on the live system has no concrete config symbol to check — the AC's named vocabulary is undefined/nonexistent, making this a T8(a) gap: a processing protocol/activation mechanism is referenced whose value vocabulary is never defined.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'The WS6-a voter is deployed + activated for the rebar Gerrit project (`gerrit_voter.enabled=true`, project allow-listed).'
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC1 as: 'The WS6-a voter receiver is deployed and running on the AWS instance (verify: `docker compose ps review-bot` shows healthy); `GERRIT_PROJECT=rebar` is set in the compose `.env`; the `project.config` ACL grants `label-LLM-Review -1..+1` to `Service Users` (verify via `infra/gerrit/project.config:20`).' This maps directly to verifiable, existing config symbols.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete deployment step to the 'What' section (e.g. the command/playbook/config-file path used to deploy the voter and set `gerrit_voter.enabled=true` for the rebar project) so AC1 maps to a traceable action.
findings.finding: The plan states the desired deployment end-state in AC1 ('voter is deployed + activated') but describes no concrete deployment step or mechanism — no script, command, playbook, or UI action — that would get an executor from the current state to the deployed state. Per E6(c), every acceptance criterion must map to at least one described step.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 / 'What' section
findings.suggested_fix: Under 'What', add a numbered step such as: '1. SSH to the rebar-MCP host (or run `ansible-playbook deploy-voter.yml -e env=prod`) and set `gerrit_voter.enabled=true` + add rebar to `gerrit_voter.projects` in `config/voter.toml`. 2. Confirm with `rebar-mcp status voter` or equivalent health-check endpoint.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out procedure (e.g., 'set `gerrit_voter.enabled=false` and remove project from allow-list') to the plan so the team has a documented recovery path if the voter misbehaves during the live dogfood run.
findings.finding: The plan activates a live voter that can veto real Gerrit changes but specifies no explicit rollback/back-out mechanism if the voter misbehaves during the dogfood run.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria / What section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection under Dependencies/Scope: 'If the voter misbehaves, disable by setting `gerrit_voter.enabled=false` and removing the rebar project from the allow-list; any spurious LLM-Review votes can be removed via `gerrit review --label LLM-Review=0`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md/design-doc entry capturing the LLM-Review voter integration: the activation contract (`gerrit_voter.enabled`, allow-list), the pipeline stage boundary (patchset-created → four-pass gate → vote), and an operational note on how to disable/rollback.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel live pipeline stage (LLM-gated Gerrit voter via rebar-MCP on AWS) but includes no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc capturing the architectural decision, integration contract, or operational runbook for this deployment.
findings.location: Scope / Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Create a brief ADR (e.g., `docs/adr/0NNN-llm-review-voter-gerrit.md`) covering: (1) decision to gate via `LLM-Review` label, (2) opt-in mechanism, (3) rollback procedure (set `gerrit_voter.enabled=false`), (4) owner/contact.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before or as part of deployment, confirm: (a) LLM/Gerrit API calls have retry/timeout and a graceful fallback (e.g., abstain vote + log) when unavailable; (b) vote writes are idempotent (duplicate webhook delivery is safe); (c) at least one observable signal (log line, metric, or alert) is in place on the live deployment so a failure during the dogfood run is diagnosable.
findings.finding: The live deployment introduces external failure points (LLM calls, Gerrit API vote writes) but the plan is entirely silent on error handling, retry/backoff, observability (metrics/logs/alerts), and fallback behavior if the LLM or Gerrit API is unavailable during or after the dogfood run.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 (end-to-end gate) and deployment scope

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback step to the AC or deployment section: 'to disable the voter set `gerrit_voter.enabled=false` and redeploy; verify no new votes are cast within N minutes' — and confirm this is tested before the proof run.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no explicit, tested rollback procedure for the live voter activation. The `gerrit_voter.enabled` flag implies a toggle exists, but no step says 'to roll back: set enabled=false, redeploy, verify votes stop' — leaving the team without a tested undo path if the voter misbehaves in production.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 / What section
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: '[ ] Rollback is verified: setting gerrit_voter.enabled=false and redeploying stops new LLM-Review votes within one patchset-created event cycle.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the owner and update process for `gerrit_voter.enabled` and the project allow-list (e.g., 'updated only via reviewed rebar-MCP config PR'), and add a RETIRE note describing how to cleanly remove the voter from the allow-list if the feature is decommissioned.
findings.finding: The `gerrit_voter.enabled` config key and project allow-list are shared/global state whose ownership, update authority, and retirement path are unspecified. If the voter is later decommissioned or the rebar project is removed from the allow-list, the plan provides no cleanup or RETIRE path for this configuration state.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 / Dependencies section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State explicitly how human administrators authenticate to the rebar-MCP service management plane (e.g., SSH with IAM-authenticated bastion, AWS SSM Session Manager, or a named admin API protected by OIDC/token auth).
findings.finding: The rebar-MCP webhook receiver is being deployed/activated as a network-reachable API service on AWS, but the plan does not state how human administrators authenticate to manage or administer that service (e.g., to change config, view logs, or disable the voter). Service-to-service credentials (the Gerrit webhook token) authenticate Gerrit to rebar-MCP, not a human/admin to the rebar-MCP management plane.
findings.location: Scope section / AC1: 'Deploy the WS6-a voter into the rebar-MCP receiver on the provisioned AWS/Gerrit environment'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or Scope note: 'Admin access to rebar-MCP config and logs is gated via AWS SSM Session Manager (IAM-authenticated); no public admin port is exposed.' If the service has no admin UI/API, state that explicitly as the no-auth rationale.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit statement of which secrets store (AWS SSM Parameter Store, Secrets Manager, or equivalent) holds the Gerrit API token and LLM API key, and how they are injected into rebar-MCP at deploy time without being committed to source control.
findings.finding: The plan requires Gerrit API credentials, an LLM API key, and likely a webhook shared secret to operate end-to-end, but nowhere specifies how these secrets are stored or injected — no reference to AWS SSM Parameter Store, Secrets Manager, Vault, or equivalent.
findings.location: What / AC1 / Dependencies section — credentials for Gerrit endpoint and LLM service are implied but unspecified

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC or Scope note specifying per-project rate limits or a cost cap for LLM API calls, and how the system behaves (fail-open / fail-closed / alerting) when the quota is exceeded.
findings.finding: The voter calls an LLM API on every `patchset-created` event with no mention of rate limits, per-project quotas, cost caps, or safeguards against runaway invocation (e.g., a CI loop pushing many patchsets rapidly).
findings.location: What section: 'patchset-created → four-pass gate → LLM-Review vote → submit'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC requiring that the live voter emits structured logs to a named log sink (e.g., CloudWatch log group) and that at least one alarm (e.g., on error rate or missing heartbeat) is configured before the deployment is considered complete.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no logging, metrics, or alerting for the live voter deployment — the only 'observability' artifact is manually recording the change URL on the ticket. There is no mention of CloudWatch logs, error alerting, or a defined teardown procedure if the voter malfunctions.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria / Scope — no observability or teardown AC

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add auth/HTTPS preconditions to the plan: name the required AWS IAM permissions (SSM `GetParameter` on `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token`), the operator SSH access model for the live host, and confirm the compose-internal delivery path (no public TLS needed for the internal `http://review-bot:8000/webhook` hop per ADR-0014).
findings.finding: The plan does not state auth/HTTPS preconditions for the live deployment step — specifically, which AWS IAM role or operator identity has permission to read the SSM parameter (`/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token`) and push config to the live host, and whether the receiver is reachable over HTTPS from Gerrit's compose network.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 / What section: 'Deploy the WS6-a voter into the rebar-MCP receiver on the provisioned AWS/Gerrit environment'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or deployment note) specifying that the Gerrit API credential used by the voter is stored in AWS Secrets Manager / Parameter Store (not plaintext in config or IaC) on the live environment.
findings.finding: The deployment plan does not specify where or how the Gerrit API credential (used by the voter to post LLM-Review votes) is stored on the live AWS environment — no mention of Secrets Manager, Parameter Store, or equivalent; a plaintext credential in config/IaC is a risk on the boundary-crossing path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC 1: 'The WS6-a voter is deployed + activated'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC 1: 'The Gerrit API token for the voter is provisioned via AWS Secrets Manager (or SSM Parameter Store SecureString) and injected at runtime — no plaintext credential in config files, environment variable literals, or IaC source.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (in the deployment plan or an AC) that the rebar-MCP receiver validates the authenticity of inbound Gerrit webhook events (e.g., HMAC signature check, shared secret, or network-level restriction to Gerrit's source IP) on the live AWS environment.
findings.finding: The plan does not address how inbound Gerrit webhook events (patchset-created) are authenticated at the rebar-MCP receiver endpoint on the live AWS deployment — a missing webhook-signature or shared-secret check would allow any actor reachable to the endpoint to inject fake review events.
findings.location: What / Scope sections — 'patchset-created → four-pass gate → LLM-Review vote'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
